Heterotropa is a genus of flowering plants in the family Acanthaceae, comprising several species of plants native to tropical regions. The health benefits of Heterotropa species are not well-documented, but they are reported to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Heterotropa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. The most common herbal preparations made with Heterotropa are infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions.
